It belongs to the genus Siphocampylus, which is a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The Campanulaceae family, also known as the bellflower family, comprises around 89 genera and over 2,300 species. intro: Siphocampylus caudatus, also known as the "Tail Flower", is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Siphocampylus caudatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Siphocampylus caudatus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
